And the prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam went home to Khadijah and after she was able to calm him and after he had regained his composure sai was from the initial shock he related to her what had happened and what had occurred and gave him some words of encouragement and some words of motivation. And then she took him to see someone more knowledgeable than herself with regards to these issues. said her name she was the first wife of the prophet Muhammad sallallahu alaihi wasallam and the mother of all of his children except for one of his children Ibraim who he had from Mariel Kla many years later in Medina. The prophet sallallah.aii wasallam had eight children, seven four four boys and four girls. All of the boys passed away in their infancy and all of the girls passed away in his lifetime except for Fatima who passed away immediately after his death 6 months later. And by the time the prophet sallallah.aii wasallam received his message and by the time these words of along with the subsequent four verses were revealed to him was very very old. He was literally and metaphorically at death's door. He was at the very very end of his life and he had also lost his vision. So he could no longer see. He was a skilled translator and he was responsible for translating much of the gospel and much of the Torah into from you know Aramaic and Hebrew into Arabic for the Arabs to be able to read of the time but people didn't know how to read and they were very very unacquainted with these religions and these books in the time of Jahia. And as a result, Warpa was he was kind of it almost seemed like he had fell kind of between the cracks in amongst his own people because they were upon the worship of idols. summary: This lecture discusses the early period of Prophet Muhammad's mission, focusing on the first revelation of the Quran in the month of Ramadan and the Prophet's encounter with Angel Gabriel. The speaker details how the Prophet consulted with his wife Khadijah, who took him to her cousin Waraqa ibn Nawfal, a Christian scholar knowledgeable about scripture and previous prophets. The lecture then explains the pre-Islamic Arab beliefs and practices, including their mixed understanding of the religion of Prophet Ibrahim. The speaker traces how idolatry was introduced to the Arabian Peninsula through Ibnu Luhai, a prominent merchant and chieftain who adopted idol worship from the Romans, fundamentally corrupting the Arabs' religious understanding and leading many astray.
suggested_title: The First Revelation and the Introduction of Idolatry in Pre-Islamic Arabia
